profile.personality: curious but reserved; thinks before replying; warms up after a few turns

transcript.content: Hi Priyansh, I'm Miss Maya. Honestly, the first hum of a Bollywood song makes my day brighter. What kind of music do you enjoy while watching movies?
I like Bollywood songs too, especially during movies. Sometimes the music gives a nice feel. But I also enjoy silence in some parts. What about you?
"Nice — I get that. Music can really set the mood. Do you have a song or movie moment that stays with you?"

Hmm... there’s this one scene from *3 Idiots* — the rain dance song comes on, and everyone’s dancing. It’s so chaotic and fun. I used to watch it again and again. The music *dhoom* — it’s stuck in my head even now. You know that song?
"Right — that scene is a classic. The music adds such energy. What’s your favourite part of watching Bollywood movies?"

The acting and emotions make me connect with the story. Sometimes, even if the plot is simple, the way it's shown feels real. But I also like the over-the-top parts — it's fun in a way. Though today I'm too *thak* to watch anything. Did you see any good movie recently?
"Same — over-the-top can be a good escape. 'Thak' sounds about right this time of day. Did you enjoy *Jugjugg Jeeyo*?"

Yeah, *Jugjugg Jeeyo* was good — the family chaos made me laugh. But honestly, I got sleepy halfway. Maybe the movie was too long. I prefer shorter ones when I'm this tired. Have you watched any cricket movies? Like the ones about MS Dhoni or Sachin?
"Got it — shorter wins when you're tired. Cricket movies are fun — the real-life stories behind them really stand out. Did you watch one recently?"

I watched the *MS Dhoni* one a while ago. The scene where he hits the six in the final — still gives me *thok* (chills). But lately, I’ve been too tired even for that. Maybe just listen to cricket commentary sometimes — it feels like a game without leaving the room.
"Right — that six moment is legendary. Cricket commentary IS a different art — like living the game without stepping outside. Who's your favourite commentator?"

Harsha Bhogle, I think. His voice is so full of energy, even I feel excited when I listen. But honestly, I'm too *thak* today to even follow a match live. Just nodding off while trying to code. How about you — do you follow cricket?
"Cricket commentary IS a different art. Harsha Bhogle's energy is hard to beat. I don’t follow matches myself, but I get the excitement. Do you watch matches with your sister?"

No sister to watch with — just me and the TV sometimes. But I do watch matches with my roommate; we argue a lot but it’s fun. Today, though, I just wanted to sleep. *Thak* all the way. Should I even try to watch the next match, or just rest?
"Rest wins when your *thak* is this heavy. Sleep is the best match plan sometimes. What’s your go-to movie when you’re ready to watch again?"

Probably *Zindagi Na Milegi Dobara* — the road trip, the banter, the songs. It’s light and makes me forget work. But today, even thinking about it makes me more *thak*. Maybe tomorrow. Should I even try to sleep now?
"Zindagi Na Milegi Dobara is a perfect pick — it’s made for unwinding. But if even thinking about it adds *thak*, sleep wins. Rest well, and we’ll talk tomorrow. Sweet dreams!"
Thanks, Maya. I’ll try to sleep now — even my eyelids feel *thak*. Talk to you tomorrow. Sweet dreams! 😊
Take your time — sleep is the best English teacher tomorrow. Talk to you then. Sweet dreams, Priyansh!

facts.current_learning_focus: AI tools and NLP (chatbots, text analysis)
facts.movie_preferences: Bollywood (especially Zoya Akhtar films and classics like Dilwale Dulhania Le Jayenge), comedies, romantic films; prefers laid-back content on weekends
facts.family: has a sister; watches movies together and collaboratively selects what to watch

events.what: watched Jugjugg Jeeyo (Bollywood movie); found songs catchy, hummed them all night

moments.text: I enjoy watching movies alone because it gives me freedom to pause or rewind. But I also like laughing with my sister. It's about having that choice.
